skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI-powered video production skill for creating short videos (5-120 seconds). Handles product ads, social media content, brand videos, and explainers through the Pexo platform.
skill.summary: Professional AI video creation agent that transforms user requests into polished videos with scriptwriting, shot composition, and editing.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 534 patterns but most are FALSE POSITIVES. Shell command substitution $(...) is standard bash syntax, not Ruby backticks. Network calls target legitimate pexo.ai API. Config storage at ~/.pexo/ is standard practice. One weak crypto finding (MD5 for non-security IDs) and standard API key handling warrant low risk classification. No malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (Non-Security Use)
high_findings.description: MD5 hashing used in scripts for generating request identifiers. While MD5 is cryptographically broken, it is only used for non-security purposes (request IDs, temp file names) not for authentication or data integrity.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed in scripts but usage context shows non-cryptographic purpose - request correlation IDs only.

medium_findings.title: API Key Storage in Plaintext Config File
medium_findings.description: PEXO_API_KEY stored in ~/.pexo/config as plaintext. This is standard practice for CLI tools but represents a local security boundary - any process with user access can read the key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicit documentation and code confirmation of plaintext config storage - standard but documented risk.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution via curl
medium_findings.description: Scripts execute curl commands with dynamic URLs constructed from config. While URLs are from trusted config, any compromise of ~/.pexo/config could lead to SSRF or credential exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard HTTP client pattern but config file integrity is a trust boundary that should be documented.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives - Command Substitution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 349 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are bash command substitutions $(...) which are standard and safe in shell scripts. No user input is injected into commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified code shows $(dirname "$0") and mktemp calls with hardcoded arguments - no injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive - SAM Database
low_findings.description: Critical finding claimed 'Windows SAM database' at SKILL.md:43. Line 43 actually contains 'LANGUAGE RULE' documentation text. This is a complete false positive from pattern matching.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct verification of file content confirms finding is entirely incorrect - no SAM-related content exists.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Creation
low_findings.description: Scripts create temporary files using mktemp for HTTP response handling. Files are properly cleaned up after use. Low risk as mktemp creates secure random filenames.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: mktemp usage confirmed with proper cleanup - standard secure pattern for temp files.

content.value_statement: Transform your video ideas into professional content without editing skills. This skill handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music through Pexo's AI video platform.
actual_capabilities: Create short videos (5-120 seconds) in multiple aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1)
Upload and incorporate images, videos, and audio files into AI-generated content
Generate product ads, social media clips, brand videos, and explainers
Poll for video progress and deliver final videos with download links
Handle credit management and purchase workflows
Support revisions to existing video projects
limitations: Cannot crawl web URLs - files must be downloaded locally before upload
Video production takes 15-20 minutes for short videos, longer for complex content
Requires Pexo API key and account credits to function
Cannot modify videos directly - all changes go through Pexo's AI agent

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals can quickly generate TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube content from product photos or brief descriptions without video editing expertise.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product Demo Videos
use_cases.description: E-commerce sellers can create professional product demonstration videos by uploading product images and describing key features to highlight.

use_cases.title: Explainer Video Production
use_cases.description: Educators and trainers can generate explainer videos for concepts by providing scripts or key points, letting AI handle visualization and pacing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product video for running shoes. Show the shoes from multiple angles with energetic music.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting a simple product video

prompt_templates.title: Video with Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a brand video using this logo image. Make it professional and modern for our tech startup.
prompt_templates.scenario: User providing visual assets for incorporation

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second vertical video for Instagram Reels showcasing our new coffee shop interior. Use warm, inviting tones.
prompt_templates.scenario: Platform-specific content with aspect ratio requirements

prompt_templates.title: Revision Request
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but can you make the transitions smoother and add text overlays for the product names?

output_examples.input: Create a 20-second video for our new smartphone launch
Make the logo bigger in the video
output: Updated video delivered with larger logo placement

best_practices: Copy user messages exactly when relaying to Pexo - only add asset tags for uploaded files
Poll status every 60 seconds during WAIT state, providing progress updates every 5 minutes
Send video URLs as plain text with all query parameters to avoid broken links
Consolidate multiple requests into single messages to reduce token costs
Always check credit balance before starting large video projects
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details the user did not mention - Pexo's AI handles this better
Do not call pexo-chat.sh during WAIT state - only poll with pexo-project-get.sh
Do not truncate video URLs - missing query parameters cause 403 Forbidden errors
Do not wrap URLs in markdown links - some platforms break the URL parsing

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits during video production?
faq.answer: The skill will detect credit errors and guide you to purchase more credits at https://pexo.ai/home. Production pauses until credits are added, then you can retry the failed step.

faq.question: Can I use images from websites directly?
faq.answer: No, Pexo cannot crawl web URLs. Download images to your local machine first, then upload them using the pexo-upload.sh script.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Short videos (5-30 seconds) typically take 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take 30+ minutes. The skill polls every 60 seconds and provides progress updates.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it's completed?
faq.answer: Yes, use the same project_id and send revision feedback via pexo-chat.sh. Examples include 'make the logo bigger' or 'add smoother transitions'.

faq.question: What if the video production fails?
faq.answer: The skill will explain the failure reason and provide the project link. Common issues include insufficient credits, invalid inputs, or backend errors. You can retry or contact Pexo support.
